summary: Microsoft documents window animation behavior through SystemParametersInfo(SPI_GETANIMATION / SPI_SETANIMATION) and ANIMATIONINFO.iMinAnimate. A local runtime diff on 2026-03-14 confirmed that calling SPI_SETANIMATION with animations enabled persisted HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop\WindowMetrics\MinAnimate = "1", while calling SPI_SETANIMATION with animations disabled persisted MinAnimate = "0". The current app write of MinAnimate = "0" therefore matches the observed persisted backend for the documented Win32 feature surface on this build.

setting.name: Disable Window Animations
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can animate windows when they open, close, minimize, or restore. Turning that off can make the interface feel snappier and reduce motion.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable animation effects when they want a faster-feeling desktop, lower visual motion, or a more minimal UI response.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling animations can make the desktop feel faster, but transitions look more abrupt. This record is validated through the official Win32 animation API plus a local API-to-registry runtime diff, not through a Microsoft-published registry contract for MinAnimate.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents ANIMATIONINFO.iMinAnimate and SystemParametersInfo(SPI_GETANIMATION / SPI_SETANIMATION) as the supported feature surface for window animations. On 2026-03-14, a local runtime diff showed SPI_SETANIMATION false leaving MinAnimate = "0" and SPI_SETANIMATION true setting MinAnimate = "1" under HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop\WindowMetrics, with the original local state restored afterward. That validates MinAnimate as the observed persisted backend for the public Win32 feature surface on this build.

allowed_values.label: Window animations disabled
allowed_values.meaning: A local SPI_SETANIMATION runtime diff confirmed that disabling the official Win32 animation feature persists MinAnimate = "0" on this build.

allowed_values.label: Window animations enabled
allowed_values.meaning: A local SPI_SETANIMATION runtime diff confirmed that enabling the official Win32 animation feature persists MinAnimate = "1" on this build.

targets.notes: Validated as an API-backed runtime registry mapping. No Microsoft page captured in this pass publishes MinAnimate directly as the supported registry contract.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app writes MinAnimate = "0" under HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop\WindowMetrics. A local runtime diff confirmed that this matches the persisted disabled state produced by the official Win32 animation API surface on this build.

windows_defaults.label: Default state not captured
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows user profiles where this pass did not capture the absent-value default for MinAnimate

states.rationale: This pass validated the persisted 0 and 1 states through the official Win32 animation API, but it did not capture the absent-value default.

windows_defaults.notes: Use this record as a validated mapping for the 0 and 1 states, not as a complete Microsoft-documented default model.

recommended_profiles.label: Disable window animations
intended_for: Users who prefer a snappier desktop feel
avoid_for: Users who prefer smoother visual transitions

states.rationale: Set the observed persisted registry backend to the disabled state proven through SPI_SETANIMATION.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want faster-feeling window transitions and less motion.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the default animated minimize and restore experience.
tradeoffs: The desktop can feel faster or more responsive.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: ANIMATIONINFO structure
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents ANIMATIONINFO.iMinAnimate as the member that controls whether minimize and restore window animations are enabled or disabled.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: SystemParametersInfo - animation actions
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents SPI_GETANIMATION and SPI_SETANIMATION as the public Win32 surface for reading and changing animation effects associated with user actions.

evidence.title: Local SPI_SETANIMATION runtime diff - MinAnimate persistence
evidence.summary: A local runtime diff called SPI_SETANIMATION with animations disabled and enabled, then read both SPI_GETANIMATION and HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop\WindowMetrics\MinAnimate after each change. Disabled stayed at API false with MinAnimate = "0". Enabled switched to API true with MinAnimate = "1". The original local state was restored at the end.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes MinAnimate = "0" under HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop\WindowMetrics.

decision.why: The feature semantics are officially documented through the Win32 animation API, and the runtime diff proves that MinAnimate is the persisted backend for the enabled and disabled states on this build. The app writes the same disabled state and supports restoring the previous value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: after_disable: API false, registry MinAnimate = "0". after_enable: API true, registry MinAnimate = "1". restored: API false, registry MinAnimate = "0".
validation_proof.notes: The local runtime diff used the official SystemParametersInfo animation API to toggle the feature and then read the persisted registry backend. The machine ended in its original state.
